location: On a corpse against the back wall of a side hallway inside the Ruined Forge Lava Intake dungeon, guarded by a second Golem enemy encountered partway through (reached by descending the first ladder, passing an initial Golem, turning right past a fire-blob enemy, then heading left down a side hallway).

source.name: corpse pickup, Ruined Forge Lava Intake (guarded by a Golem)

tactics: From the Castle Front grace (at the Castle Ensis legacy dungeon entrance), ride southeast past Ellac Greatbridge to reach the Ruined Forge Lava Intake grace at the dungeon mouth
Descend the first ladder into the forge and fight/dodge past a stone Golem, then turn right after passing a fire-blob enemy

Look left down a side hallway for a second Golem guarding the corpse — these Golems hit hard and can grab/crush at close range, so bait their slow swings or chip them from range rather than trading blows
The dagger sits on the corpse along with a Smithing Stone [4]; no boss fight or lever puzzle is required, just navigation past the two Golems
lore: A dagger etched with a smithing incantation that lightens its mass, letting the weapon's 'smithscript' properties turn every attack into a thrown strike that boomerangs straight back to the wielder's hand instead of staying lodged in the target.

builds.name: Dex bleed/ranged poke
builds.why: Dexterity is by far the dominant scaling stat (C-grade vs E-grade in Str/Int/Fai), and as a Throwing Blade it attacks entirely at range for zero FP cost while still applying Piercing Throw for extra reach — good for a hybrid melee/ranged Dex build that wants free chip damage without spending FP

builds.name: Quality/hybrid utility
builds.why: Small secondary scaling in Str, Int, and Fai plus full infusability means it can slot Ashes of War or elemental affinities to patch a hybrid build's damage type, even though Dex remains the priority stat
